# ESPHome constraints files (SC.3)

**One `<version>.txt` per supported ESPHome release**, generated by
`pip-compile --generate-hashes --strip-extras` inside the
`python:3.11-slim` / linux/amd64 base image (same host the worker
builds against).

## Why these exist

Without this, every ESPHome install on a worker resolves + downloads
the latest compatible version of every transitive dep from PyPI, with
no cryptographic pin — a compromised PyPI package (or mirror) lands
as arbitrary Python code running inside the worker container, which
has network access to every ESP device on the LAN. With a hash-pinned
constraints file, each `pip install --require-hashes` run refuses to
install any wheel whose SHA-256 doesn't match what we committed here.

## How the worker uses them

`ha-addon/client/version_manager.py::_install(version)` checks for a
file at `ha-addon/client/esphome-constraints/<version>.txt`:

- **File present** — the install runs as
  ``pip install --require-hashes -c <version>.txt esphome==<version>``.
  Anything not in the constraints file fails the install. This is
  the preferred path.
- **File missing** — the install runs unpinned (the pre-SC.3
  behavior) and logs a WARNING so operators know hash-pinning wasn't
  enforced for that version. Keeps older ESPHome versions from being
  locked out by the upgrade, but surfaces the security gap in logs.
  Future releases may flip this to a hard refusal (see SECURITY_AUDIT
  F-18 for the roadmap).

The constraints file ships **inside the worker Docker image**, so a
worker that auto-updates its source without pulling a new image
still has the correct constraints (the `.txt` files are copied into
the image at build time via `ha-addon/client/Dockerfile`).

## Regenerating

Use `scripts/regen-esphome-constraints.sh <version>` — it runs
`pip-compile --generate-hashes --strip-extras` in a Linux/amd64
Docker container (same shape as `scripts/refresh-deps.sh`) so the
resulting hashes don't leak macOS-only transitives (PY-9).

The release cadence is: when ESPHome ships a new release that we
want to support, regenerate + commit the `.txt` file and bump the
worker `IMAGE_VERSION` so the new constraints ship to every worker.
A scheduled GitHub Action that opens a PR weekly with newly-
generated constraints for any recently-published ESPHome version
automates this — see `.github/workflows/regen-esphome-constraints.yml`.
